ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 (OceanLotus / Cobalt Kitty) Office-to-mshta-to-PowerShell HTA delivery chain
ucs.description: Hunts the signature OceanLotus initial-access chain documented in Cybereason's Cobalt Kitty report: a maldoc spawns mshta.exe with a remote .hta payload (or a JavaScript/VBScript moniker), which then drops to PowerShell/cmd within a couple of minutes. The 60-120s parent->mshta->scripting-host stitch is what differentiates APT32 from generic mshta abuse.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as mshta_time values(Processes.process) as mshta_cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=mshta.exe AND Processes.parent_process_name IN (winword.exe,excel.exe,powerpnt.exe,outlook.exe,chrome.exe,msedge.exe,firefox.exe) AND (Processes.process="*.hta*" OR Processes.process="*http*" OR Processes.process="*javascript:*" OR Processes.process="*vbscript:*") by Processes.dest Processes.user | rename Processes.* as * | join type=inner dest user [| tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as child_time values(Processes.process_name) as child_proc values(Processes.process) as child_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN (powershell.exe,pwsh.exe,cmd.exe,rundll32.exe,regsvr32.exe,wscript.exe,cscript.exe) AND Processes.parent_process_name=mshta.exe by Processes.dest Processes.user] | eval delta=child_time-mshta_time | where delta>=0 AND delta<=120 | table mshta_time child_time delta dest user parent_proc mshta_cmd child_proc child_cmd
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 120;
let Mshta = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName =~ "mshta.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".hta","http://","https://","javascript:","vbscript:")
    | project MshtaTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              MshtaCmd = ProcessCommandLine, ParentFile = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "mshta.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| join kind=inner Mshta on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (MshtaTime .. MshtaTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project MshtaTime, ChildTime = Timestamp,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, MshtaTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName, ParentFile, ParentCmd, MshtaCmd,
          ChildFile = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine, ChildSha256 = SHA256
| order by MshtaTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 120;
let Mshta = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\mshta.exe"
    | where ParentProcessName endswith_any ("\\winword.exe","\\excel.exe","\\powerpnt.exe","\\outlook.exe","\\chrome.exe","\\msedge.exe","\\firefox.exe","\\iexplore.exe")
    | where CommandLine has_any (".hta","http://","https://","javascript:","vbscript:")
    | project MshtaTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              MshtaCmd = CommandLine, ParentProcessName,
              MshtaPid = NewProcessId;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\mshta.exe"
| where NewProcessName endswith_any ("\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe","\\cmd.exe","\\rundll32.exe","\\regsvr32.exe","\\wscript.exe","\\cscript.exe")
| join kind=inner Mshta on Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (MshtaTime .. MshtaTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project MshtaTime, ChildTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, MshtaTime),
          Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, MshtaCmd,
          ChildProcess = NewProcessName, ChildCmd = CommandLine
| order by MshtaTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT32 OceanLotus Office to mshta HTA Delivery
id: 7c4f9c11-1c6e-4e6d-b0a5-4a8a1c8f2a31
status: experimental
description: Detects APT32/OceanLotus initial-access tradecraft - Office or browser process spawning mshta.exe with an HTA URL or scripting moniker, characteristic of the Cobalt Kitty campaign.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0050/
  - https://www.cybereason.com/blog/operation-cobalt-kitty-apt
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.g0050
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith: '\mshta.exe'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\iexplore.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '.hta'
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
      - 'javascript:'
      - 'vbscript:'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate enterprise HTA-based applications launched from Office (rare)
level: high
ucs.rationale: OceanLotus's hallmark from Cobalt Kitty (Cybereason 2017) and subsequent Mandiant/CrowdStrike (Ocean Buffalo) reporting is Word/Outlook macro -> mshta.exe -> staged PowerShell, dropping Denis or Cobalt Strike. The triple constraint of Office-or-browser parent + mshta + scripting-host child within 120s strips out admin-installed HTA tools and isolates this APT32 chain specifically (T1218.005 + T105

_field_issues.message: `MshtaTime` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ParentFile` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ParentCmd` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`MshtaCmd` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `MshtaTime` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`MshtaCmd` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.

ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 DLL side-loading via signed McAfee/Symantec/Avast/Google binary running from user-writable path
ucs.description: Hunts APT32/OceanLotus's most distinctive persistence/defence-evasion pattern: dropping a signed third-party security/utility binary (McAfee mcoemcpl/wsc_proxy, Symantec rastlsc, Avast wsc_proxy, AVG, Google goopdate, Adobe AcroTranscoder, RoboForm) into AppData/ProgramData/Public/Temp and side-loading a malicious DLL adjacent to it. Catches Denis, KOMPROGO, Roland, Ratsnif loaders.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN (wsc_proxy.exe,mcoemcpl.exe,mcvsshld.exe,mcods.exe,rastlsc.exe,goopdate.exe,googleupdate.exe,acrotranscoder.exe,roboform.exe,wercplsupport.exe,winwhd.exe,syssetup.exe) AND (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*") AND NOT (Processes.process_path="C:\\Program Files*" OR Processes.process_path="C:\\Windows*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path | rename Processes.* as * | sort 0 - first_seen
ucs.kql:
let _SideLoadedBins = dynamic([
    "wsc_proxy.exe","mcoemcpl.exe","mcvsshld.exe","mcods.exe",
    "rastlsc.exe","goopdate.exe","googleupdate.exe",
    "acrotranscoder.exe","roboform.exe","wercplsupport.exe",
    "winwhd.exe","syssetup.exe"
]);
let _LegitVendors = dynamic([
    "McAfee","Symantec","Avast","AVG","Adobe","Google",
    "Norton","Siber Systems","Kaspersky"
]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (_SideLoadedBins)
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows"
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\", @"\Temp\", @"\Users\")
| where ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (_LegitVendors)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | project DllTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId, LoadedDll = FileName,
              LoadedDllPath = FolderPath, LoadedDllSha = SHA256
  ) on DeviceId, $left.ProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| where LoadedDllPath =~ FolderPath and LoadedDllSha != SHA256
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          BinaryName = FileName, BinaryPath = FolderPath,
          BinaryVendor = ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName, BinarySha = SHA256,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentFile = InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          LoadedDll, LoadedDllSha
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _SideLoadedBins = dynamic([
    "wsc_proxy.exe","mcoemcpl.exe","mcvsshld.exe","mcods.exe",
    "rastlsc.exe","goopdate.exe","googleupdate.exe",
    "acrotranscoder.exe","roboform.exe","wercplsupport.exe",
    "winwhd.exe","syssetup.exe"
]);
let _LegitVendors = dynamic([
    "McAfee","Symantec","Avast","AVG","Adobe","Google",
    "Norton","Siber Systems","Kaspersky"
]);
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| extend ImageBase = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ImageBase in (_SideLoadedBins)
| where TargetProcessName !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
| where TargetProcessName !startswith @"C:\Windows"
| where TargetProcessName has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\", @"\Temp\", @"\Users\")
| where TargetProcessFileCompany has_any (_LegitVendors)
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          BinaryPath = TargetProcessName,
          BinaryVendor = TargetProcessFileCompany,
          BinarySha = SHA256,
          TargetProcessCommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, ParentProcessCommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT32 DLL Side-Loading via Signed Vendor Binary in User-Writable Path
id: 9b9a1d36-1f2a-4f0c-b6a1-4f17aa2e7d8e
status: experimental
description: Detects APT32/OceanLotus DLL side-loading by execution of a signed third-party vendor binary (McAfee, Symantec, Avast, AVG, Google, Adobe, Siber Systems) from a user-writable directory - characteristic of OceanLotus loaders for Denis, KOMPROGO, Roland, Ratsnif.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0050/
  - https://www.cybereason.com/blog/operation-cobalt-kitty-apt
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2018/03/13/oceanlotus-ships-new-backdoor/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0050
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\wsc_proxy.exe'
      - '\McOemCpl.exe'
      - '\McVsShld.exe'
      - '\McODS.exe'
      - '\RasTlsC.exe'
      - '\AcroTranscoder.exe'
      - '\GoogleUpdate.exe'
      - '\goopdate.exe'
      - '\RoboForm.exe'
      - '\winwhd.exe'
      - '\wercplsupport.exe'
  selection_user_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Public\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
  filter_legit:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
      - 'C:\Windows\'
  condition: selection_image and selection_user_path and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Portable installers extracted to Temp by helpdesk
level: high
ucs.rationale: DLL side-loading using signed third-party security-vendor binaries is the OceanLotus signature - Cybereason's Cobalt Kitty report, ESET WeLiveSecurity 2018, FireEye M-Trends 2017 all document this exact pattern (rastlsc/wsc_proxy/mcoemcpl) for the Denis/KOMPROGO/Roland/Ratsnif/Salgorea backdoors. Pinning the path to AppData/ProgramData/Temp removes the legitimate vendor-installed copies and isolat
